title: Nepal's former PM Oli arrested over deaths during Gen Z protests
description: Police spokesperson Om ‌Adhikari confirmed the detainment of ​K.P. ​Sharma Oli and his Home Minister, Ramesh Lekhak; a panel ⁠which investigated violence ⁠during the anti-corruption protests recommended that Mr. Oli be prosecuted for ​failing to ‌prevent the crackdown on the protests

Rohit Sharma is chilling and pulling: 15 kilos gone, captaincy anxieties gone this IPL
Long queues at UP petrol pumps for second day in a row, authorities reassure stable fuel supply
Anger on the banks of the Musi
description: With the Telangana government ready to implement the Musi Riverfront Development Project in Hyderabad, residents along the river are facing possible displacement and uncertainty over compensation. The Moosa and Esi tributaries, once seasonal streams, now flow with sewage and industrial effluents, raising questions about ecological restoration. Swathi Vadlamudi speaks to citizens and environmentalists

title: Elon Musk joined Trump and PM Modi call to discuss US-Israel war against Iran, says report
Canadian tourists lists out ‘normal daily things’ that they found fascinating
description: Vancouver couple Nick and Raychel discovered India's simple yet logical daily practices. They observed fennel seeds ending meals, the extensive train network connecting the nation, and the balanced thali meals. Hand wash stations were readily available. Clay cups for chai offered a unique flavor and eco-friendly disposal. Leaf plates served street food.

title: Morning Digest: PM calls for ‘Team India’ effort in meet with CMs over West Asia crisis; 5 India-bound LPG ships stay anchored near Hormuz Strait, and more
description: Here is a select list of stories to start the day

title: Watch: US President calls it ‘Strait of Trump,’ then says it wasn’t an accident
description: US president Trump playfully referred to the Strait of Hormuz as the "Strait of Trump" during a Miami speech, drawing laughter. The comment comes amid heightened tensions over the critical oil shipping route, which Iran has the ability to disrupt. Trump's remarks align with his history of branding and a reported consideration to rename the strait.

title: US-Iran war, Day 29:  Trump signals progress in talks, Iran denies negotiations amid escalating conflict
Trump says ‘PM Modi and I get things done’, Iran thanks India for ‘support during response to US attack’
Indus Water Treaty suspension speeding up projects, govt says J-K hydropower capacity to rise 46%
Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Wildlife incursion, a vote-catcher issue in many constituencies
description: Wildlife attacks have claimed many lives and ruined farmers financially. So affected are the farmers that they have fielded a candidate in Kozhikode against the State Forest Minister. They are seeking the views of candidates across all constituencies regarding conflict mitigation plans. In at least one-fourth of the constituencies in the State, it is the main topic of electioneering

title: Love, longing, loss in Bastar
Software ‘ready’, office space to be ‘finalised’: Bengal SIR hits tribunal bump now
Amid Iran war, Trump says he’d love his legacy to be made as ‘great peacemaker’

title: Aurangabad horror: Uncle locks 3 children inside room, slits their throats, attempts suicide

title: Ex-Nepal PM KP Sharma Oli arrested over role in Gen Z protest crackdown
description: Former Nepal Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and Home Minister Ramesh Lekhak were arrested in a culpable homicide case linked to the September Gen Z protests. Police acted on a commission's report recommending charges for criminal negligence, which resulted in 77 deaths and significant property damage. The arrests follow the swearing-in of new Prime Minister Balendra Shah.

‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ collection: Movie surpasses Rs 1,100 cr worldwide
description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has shattered box office records, achieving a staggering Rs 1100 crore worldwide. The spy thriller garnered a massive opening, surpassing Rs 800 crore gross in India within nine days. Despite a slight weekday dip, the film's impressive performance continues to captivate audiences, with expectations of a weekend surge.

title: ‘I would love to add some bling to your teeth’: Rishab Rikhiram Sharma reveals story behind his diamond smile

title: Panel: Take steps to protect farmers against cheap edible oil imports

37 lakh voters adjudicated as EC publishes second supplementary list for Bengal
'Laanat hai zindagi par': Yograj Singh's sharp jibe at Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli
description: Former cricketer Yograj Singh has ignited a debate on player longevity, criticizing Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma for contemplating retirement. He believes performance should dictate selection, not age.  Interestingly, Singh lauded MS Dhoni's continued fitness and dedication, strongly opposing calls for his retirement and advocating for his extended playing career.

title: War in West Asia: Gold prices crash on inflation concerns, dollar emerges as safe bet

Delhi Confidential: MoS’s Memento
EC seeks report on Mamata’s ‘provocative’ remark from CEO
description: With her statement seen as a provocation to TMC women workers and supporters to launch attacks at polling stations in rural areas, posing a risk to the central armed police forces deployed there, of facing criminal charges were they to intervene and stop them, EC will examine if the statement amounts of provocation and interferes in discharge of duties by poll and security personnel.

title: Chakankar quits as chief of NCP women’s wing amid Kharat row
HC junks plea seeking CBI probe into Adani Green bribery claims
Pilots of VIP charters can now refuse to fly in unsafe conditions
description: Noting that VVIPs “use air travel frequently for electioneering and other purposes in (small) aircraft” and choppers, DGCA said, “Analysis of earlier accidents/incidents associated with aircraft operations to/from airstrips/temporary helipads and during election flying has often revealed violation of instructions and safety was jeopardised. It is essential that adequate measures are taken by all concerned to ensure the highest standards of safety for operation of such flights.”

title: Clashes erupt during Ram Navami procession in Murshidabad, prohibitory orders imposed
12 lakh on Bengal’s 2nd supplementary list, but no clarity on who made the cut
description: As was the case when Election Commission released the first supplementary rolls close to midnight last Monday, there were more questions than CEO Manoj Agarwal had answers for. “About 37 lakh of the 60 lakh pending cases have been disposed of till now. After receiving the list from Calcutta HC, EC will take 4-6 hours to process and upload it on our server,” he said in the afternoon.

title: US, Ukraine citizens who went to Mizoram posed no threat to India: Shah
description: This is the first time a govt functionary has clarified on record that the group’s interest lay in Myanmar and India was merely a transit point. “No threat was posed to India’s security. It is our policy that any foreigner who visits India for any wrong act will not be spared,” he said.

title: Govt clears Rs 2.4 lakh crore proposals for procurement of more S-400 units
description: The approval for additional S-400 ‘Sudarshan’ systems, which had helped neutralise Pakistani missiles during last year’s conflict in May and had also to its credit the world’s longest-range kill of an enemy aircraft, has come at a time when IAF teams are in Russia to receive the fourth squadron under the earlier contract for five such systems. The fourth system is likely to arrive by May while the fifth squadron is expected by Dec.

title: Sambhal next major religious hub? UP govt floats plan to revive pilgrimage route, 87 teerths
Transgender advisors who quit council: Were kept in dark about Bill, minister skipped meet
'Alive & protesting’: MP in Janata regime turns 100
description: Sitting in his Bharatpur home, the veteran, “Lohia’s oldest disciple” as he likes to call himself, speaks with the authority of one who has not just witnessed history, but shaped it. Until a recent fall briefly hospitalised him, he was holding three meetings a week. What keeps him going is unfinished work.

title: Rs 2.38 lakh cr-defence push: DAC clears 5 S-400 missile systems from Russia, transport aircraft
‘Evidence insufficient’: Spl Delhi court clears ex-MP Darda, son & others in coal ‘scam’ case
description: The case stemmed from the allocation in Maharashtra’s Bandar and led to the first CBI chargesheet in the larger coal block controversy dating back to the time Congress-led UPA was in office. The FIR was filed in 2012 and the chargesheet in 2014. Judge Sunena Sharma held that there was no proof of criminal conspiracy, noting the case was based on conjectures and lacked evidence of a “meeting of minds” or illegal agreement.

title: Supreme Court rebukes courts for naming rape survivors
description: A bench of Justices Sanjay Karol and N K Singh expressed concern that despite statutory safeguards and repeated directions from SC, such lapses persist. The court made the observation while convicting a rape accused, noting that the name of the minor survivor was mentioned in lower court orders. ​The court rejected the argument of the woman’s family.

title: Jewar in the crown: PM to open NCR’s 2nd international airport today
description: The PM is scheduled to arrive at the airport at 11.30am on Saturday, go on a walkthrough of the terminal building, and formally inaugurate the airport around noon before addressing a public gathering. The event is expected to draw large crowds from Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Bulandshahr and other parts of western UP. Three pandals have been set up to accommodate more than one lakh people.

title: Indian ships face GPS jams, mine alerts in Hormuz
description: Ships in the region are reporting repeated radio broadcasts over maritime channels warning vessels against transit. “Attention all ships… This is the Sepah Navy… navigation through Strait of Hormuz is forbidden… no ship is allowed to pass until further notice,” the radio messages repeatedly blare. Crews attributed these announcements to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy.

title: Commercial LPG allocation to states increased by 20%
description: In a letter to chief secretaries of states and UTs, petroleum secretary Neeraj Mittal said only those industrial units that have registered with oil marketing companies, declaring the end use of gas cylinders, and have also signed up for the piped gas network will be eligible for the additional quota of LPG. He added that if LPG is used by certain industrial units for special purposes that cannot be substituted by natural gas, such requirements would stand waived.

title: Clashes erupt during Ram Navami procession in Bengal's Murshidabad, security tightened
description: The situation turned volatile after an altercation during the procession escalated into violence, with both sides throwing stones at each other and vandalising property in the area

title: Lockdown reports‘harmful, baseless’, says govt, allays fear
description: Home minister Shah accused opposition parties of spreading panic over the West Asia crisis, including the claim that a lockdown may be imposed. “I want to assure everyone. Do not fear, nothing will happen. We have adequate reserves of petroleum products,” he said. While many countries had taken emergency measures, India was working to tide over the crisis without compromising the daily lives of people, he said, adding that it had ensured regular gas supply to homemakers.

title: Tiger Woods arrested on DUI charge after Florida rollover crash
description: Woods was involved in a serious rollover car crash in California that left him with severe right leg injuries in 2021

title: NCERT reset, AI push: Pradhan spells out education roadmap
description: On the recent controversy involving UGC regulations aimed at maintaining standards, quality and equity in Indian higher education, Pradhan refrained from detailed comment, noting the matter is sub judice. “The govt’s responsibility is to ensure that no one faces injustice or discrimination. We will abide by whatever directions court issues,” he said. Outlining the ministry’s broader priorities, Pradhan said the education system currently serves around 30 crore students, placing significant responsibility on policymakers.

title: Defence, security at core of India-US ties: Sergio Gor
description: In an interview with the US embassy publication, Span magazine, the ambassador also spoke about the significance of Quad as he predicted further strengthening of defence ties. “India is a Major Defence Partner, and defence cooperation is one of the brightest spots in our bilateral relationship. We also strengthen US-India military interoperability through participation in military exercises such as Malabar, Tiger Triumph, and Cope India,” said Gor.

title: Fertilizer stock ample, working to ensure supply intact: Chouhan
5 India-bound LPG ships stay anchored near Hormuz Strait
description: Appearing to bunch together so they can troop out of the strait, these ships carry more than 1.7 lakh tonnes of LPG

title: Freedom of navigation important for global economic security: EAM Jaishankar
description: Jaishankar said on X he raised Global South’s concerns about energy challenges, fertiliser supplies and food security in the session on global governance. “The uncertainties arising from the conflict in West Asia only make a stronger case for more resilient trade corridors and supply chains,” he said. Jaishankar said on X India’s FTAs with the EU, EFTA members and the UK have enhanced the utility of IMEC.

title: Diabetic drug metformin found to act on brain
description: A study published in Science Advances by Baylor College of Medicine has uncovered a hidden brain pathway that helps explain how metformin lowers blood sugar, offering fresh insight into a medicine used for over six decades. For years, metformin was believed to act mainly on the liver and gut. The new research shows it also works through the ventromedial hypothalamus — a small but crucial brain region that controls hunger, energy use and glucose balance.

title: Jan Vishwas bill moots fine, jail for grabbing govt land
description: The bill — introduced in Lok Sabha on Friday — proposes to amend Section-11 of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorised Occupants) Act that at present provides for six months’ jail or with Rs 5,000 penalty or both. As per the proposal, the fine would be 5% of land value for every year of occupation or both — penalty and jail. Officials said this would deter encroachment and check land grabbing.

title: ‘Iran will never go nuclear, India can help end this war,’ says Khamenei’s representative
description: “Our supreme leader issued a fatwa. He said nuclear weapons are forbidden, haraam. So, we don't have it. We don't want to have it. We are not looking for it,” said Ilahi. Ending the war in West Asia depends on countries that started it, he said. He dismissed Pakistan’s claim that it was mediating between Iran and the US.

title: Karur MP lashes out at TNCC high command over seat-sharing talks
Unite to face West Asia crisis, PM tells CMs; urges to boost people’s confidence
description: While he focused on preparing for prolonged disruption of supply lines, Modi also conveyed assurance about the country being in good stead, saying “efforts...in recent years to strengthen India’s economic and supply systems have proved beneficial”. In his virtual meeting with CMs — except those of Assam, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Puducherry where assembly polls are due next month — on Friday, PM Modi said while addressing a challenge is a shared responsibility.

title: Govt slashes excise duty on petrol, diesel to shield consumers, oil companies
description: The windfall tax will also help the Centre partly offset the impact of the excise duty cut. Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs chairman Vivek Chaturvedi said the windfall tax on exports will result in a gain of about Rs 1,500 crore in the first fortnight while the govt will have to forgo over Rs 7,000 crore in revenue because of the excise duty cut. He said the export tax will be reviewed fortnightly.

title: Assembly approves budget 2026-27; Delhi will now run on results, not excuses: CM
description: The Assembly also passed the Societies Registration (Delhi Amendment) Bill, 2026

title: Italy beats Northern Ireland 2-0 in World Cup playoff semifinal. Gyökeres gets hat trick for Sweden
description: The Azzurri beat Northern Ireland 2-0 in the European semifinals

title: Elon Musk joined Trump's call with PM Modi on Iran war earlier this week: Report
description: The conversation focused on the evolving situation in West Asia, with both leaders emphasising the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, secure and accessible. The key maritime corridor handles a significant share of global oil shipments and remains critical to international energy markets. However, it remains unclear why Musk was included in the call or whether he spoke.

title: Fans react as Ayesha Curry’s 'disrespectful' comment about Stephen Curry
description: Ayesha Curry's innocent childhood anecdote about not wanting to date athletes, shared on a podcast, was misconstrued online as a present-day insult to husband Stephen Curry.  The internet's harsh reaction, fueled by a lack of context, transformed a lighthearted story about teenage misconceptions into a viral controversy, sparking debates about their marriage and Steph's choices.

title: Chhattisgarh High Court says signs, gestures are oral evidence, upholds conviction of man for raping disabled woman
description: Court observes that the trial court had adopted demonstrative methods, including the use of a plastic doll, to remove any possibility of misunderstanding in the survivor’s testimony

title: Varsities ‘mismanaged’ during AAP tenure: Sood
North, central Delhi to get water supply only once a day amid WTP glitch

description: The poll panel described it as "serious negligence and dereliction of duty" on the part of the officer.

NIA granted 45-day extension in Red Fort blast probe
description: The NIA, in its plea, cited the need for further investigation in light of the recent arrests of Zamir Ahmed Ahangar and Tufail Ahmad Bhat

title: IIT Guwahati team develops energy-efficient bricks
description: The researchers said these bricks have been designed to keep buildings naturally cool, offering a solution for sustainable construction

T.N. government releases draft State Mental Healthcare Regulations, 2026
description: The Tamil Nadu State Mental Health Authority will maintain a register of practising mental health professionals in the State that will be updated monthly. It also proposes to publish a district-wise list of mental health professionals on January 1 every year

title: ED charges ex-RCOM executive Punit Garg in ₹40,000-crore bank loan fraud case
description: Chargesheet also names Vaishali Jairam Mane as an accused, for her alleged involvement in the purchase of a luxury Manhattan apartment from the proceeds of the crime

title: Supreme Court flags repeated breaches of law on disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity
description: Supreme Court warns of “general indifference” as case records reveal disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity, violating IPC Section 228A

description: External Affairs Minister stresses importance of resilient trade corridors, such as the IMEC, especially amidst uncertainties of West Asian conflict; calls for freedom of navigation to ensure global economic security

title: India signs ₹858-crore defence deals with Russian, U.S. firms
description: Ministry to procure air defence systems from Russia and get Navy fleet maintenance support from U.S.

title: People spreading rumours on lockdown will be booked: CM Fadnavis
description: He emphasised that we have enough stock of petrol and diesel to last for a month and appealed to citizens not to line up outside the petrol pump

title: Delhi HC protects Polo Ground and Race Club from eviction for now
description: The court restrained the Centre from taking coercive action based on eviction notices issued on March 12, 2026, concerning the Jaipur Polo Ground in the Race Course area, and the Delhi Race Club at Kamal Ataturk Marg

title: T.N. Assembly election: EC orders State government to post new SPs for 4 districts, including Kancheepuram
description: It adds that the officers transferred out must not be posted in any poll-related posts till the completion of the election

title: T.N. Assembly election: negotiations with DMK to identify constituencies for VCK completed, says Thirumavalavan
description: He says the constituencies allotted to his party will be announced soon

description: Police said the arrested farmer was a resident of Athur Kuppam village near Natrampalli town in the district

Preparedness meeting held for Chithirai festival
BMC barricades Mumbai’s first musical road
description: The tune of ‘Jai Ho’ from the film Slumdog Millionaire is played as a motorist passes from the dedicated stretch on the underground tunnel at Breach Candy

description: Rebel candidate Sadiq Pahilwan announced that he would extend full support to Congress candidate Samarth Mallikarjun, son of Minister S.S. Mallikarjun

title: Congress terms fuel price reduction as a ploy to help oil firms and cautions of a hike after polls
Karnataka High Court quashes FIR against Sri Sri Ravishankar on land encroachment allegations
description: There is not even an iota of allegation against the petitioner for land grabbing or encroachment, observes Justice M. Nagaprasanna

title: T.N. Assembly election: AIADMK fields new face in Sengottaiyan’s stronghold of Gobichettipalayam
description: V.B. Prabhu, who joined the party in 2002, rose through the ranks to serve as deputy secretary of the party’s student wing for a decade. Over the past six months, he was the West Union secretary in the constituency

title: TVK chief Vijay accuses Chennai police, officials of denying permission for his Perambur campaign
description: “Every time I plan an election campaign, the DMK government continues to delay or deny permission citing various reasons,” he stated.

title: Cyber Crime Wing launches election awareness video
Colitis & Crohn’s Foundation launches Tamil Nadu chapter to boost IBD awareness
description: CCFI noted a higher prevalence of Crohn’s disease in southern India, with paediatric cases accounting for nearly 10% of the disease burden in Tamil Nadu

title: Rajasthan bags national award on completion of irrigation census
description: The process was accelerated through the training sessions and workshops organised at various levels

title: History-sheeter held for train robberies, police recover gold jewellery
Four persons sentenced to life imprisonment for kidnapping, murdering marine engineer in 2012
Govt. notifies TRP-2026; tightens audit norms, expands sample size
description: Under the new policy, the net-worth requirement for a company willing to register as TV rating agency has been reduced from existing ₹20 crore to ₹5 crore

title: Residents want closure of waste disposal facility and restoration of Thamarai lake
description: Some of the burning issues faced by residents of Gummidipoondi constituency are the expansion of the industrial waste disposal facility in SIPCOT industrial complex and health hazards, discharge of sewage into Thamarai lake, lack of public transport facilities, and poor health infrastructure

title: Electors pitch for business development, peace and harmony in Harbour Assembly constituency
T.N. Assembly election: AIADMK releases second list of candidates, retains 30 sitting legislators
description: Prominent among those who did not figure in the list are former Ministers V. Saroja, V.M. Rajalakshmi, Vaigaichelvan, and ‘Mafoi’ K. Pandiarajan, all of whom lost in the 2021 election

title: 7 persons, including police inspector, get four-year jail term in income tax fraud case
Three warning signs of osteoporosis men frequently ignore
Sabrina Carpenter’s bold stance resurfaces as Shaquille O'Neal battles fake DM rumors
description: Shaquille O'Neal, the NBA legend, has found himself in a mess after he was accused of sending inappropriate texts to popular pop star Sabrina Carpenter.This news has sparked panic among fans as many troll Shaquille O'Neal for allegedly pursuing Sabrina Carpenter despite their massive age gap, with alleged inappropriate texts. Amid this, popstar Sabrina Carpenter’s powerful words have resurfaced on her moves in her concerts.

title: EC seeks West Bengal CEO’s report on Mamata speech
NIA court refuses consular access to 7 foreigners, extends custody by 10 days
Govt’s new TV Ratings Policy defines rules for accountability, transparency
UK MP says US report on rise in anti-Indian hate online ‘deeply concerning’
description: Blackman, who represents Harrow East, where Hindus were recently attacked celebrating Holi, said of the report: “This is a coordinated campaign driven by a small number of accounts deliberately targeting the Indian community, its culture, and its contribution to public life. It is racism, plain and simple. The Indian diaspora here in Harrow, across the UK, and the world, makes an extraordinary contribution to our societies. That contribution deserves celebration. I will always stand against any form of discrimination targeting our Indian community."

title: Maharashtra woman, ex-lover poison cop dad’s milkshake, arrested 3 years later
description: A Chandrapur head constable's death, initially ruled an accident, has been reclassified as premeditated murder. Investigators arrested his daughter, a police trainee, and three others, including her estranged partner who turned whistleblower. The daughter allegedly poisoned her father's milkshake, leading to his collapse on duty. The motive appears to stem from her father's opposition to her relationship.

title: Mirwaiz meets Iranian envoy, calls Kashmir ‘little Iran’
description: The delegation condemned the war, which it said was “imposed” on Iran, and expressed hope that diplomatic efforts would prevail and peace and stability would be restored. Others who were part of the delegation included Mufti Nasir-ul-Islam, Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosvi, and Aga Syed Hadi AlMosvi, Aga Syed Mujtaba. During the meeting, Mirwaiz also highlighted the “deep-rooted cultural, historical and religious ties between Kashmir and Iran”.

title: 7 killed as avalanche buries several vehicles at Zojila Pass
description: The Zojila pass would earlier remain closed for nearly five months during winter, cutting off Ladakh from the rest of the country. However, with improved technology, advanced snow-clearance equipment and better planning, the pass remained open throughout this winter, barring four days, which the Border Roads Organisation described as a significant strategic and logistical achievement.

title: Hydel power projects in J&K on fast track after Indus treaty abeyance: Omar
description: India last year placed the IWT in abeyance after the Pahalgam terror attack as part of a series of measures announced by PM Narendra Modi, including the suspension of the decades-old water-sharing pact. The treaty was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960 for sharing of Indus river system waters. According to the treaty, India got the full rights for utilization of waters of the three eastern rivers — Ravi, Beas and Satluj — while Pakistan got rights over the three western rivers, Indus, Chenab and Jhelum.

title: High-altitude unit spawned by Pahalgam attack, trained by AP’s Greyhounds
description: Snow Leopards were raised to strengthen high-altitude operations, including mountain tops and ridges, the official said. The Markhor batches are trained for forests and rugged mountainous areas long used by terrorists as hideouts. Its name is derived from a Persian term meaning “snake eater,” symbolising agility and resilience.  Both units are equipped with modern weapons and communication systems.

description: Iranian-linked hackers, Handala Hack Team, have breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email, releasing personal photos and documents. While the FBI confirmed the breach, they downplayed its significance, stating no government information was compromised. The group, believed to be a front for Iranian intelligence, previously claimed responsibility for hacking medical equipment manufacturer Stryker.

Kaif emphasized that a fitter Rohit should be involved in all game phases.

title: Top executives of two biggest US companies are leaving and they say the reason is AI
description: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta is rebranding employee titles and roles as it goes AI-native
description: Meta's Reality Labs is undergoing a significant internal overhaul, rebranding employees as 'AI builders' and forming smaller, AI-focused teams. This strategic shift aims to boost engineering productivity and product quality, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg envisioning AI dramatically transforming work.  These new 'AI pods' will foster cross-functional collaboration, with dedicated leads managing operations and performance.
